input_received.original_request: Daily content pipeline — first run. No prior artifacts.

keyword_rationale.search_volume_estimate: high — LangGraph alone: 27,100 monthly searches; CrewAI: 14,800
keyword_rationale.trend_rationale: Reddit r/AI_Agents declares 2026 'the year of multi-agent architectures, not single-agent systems.' Multiple sources (Codebridge, Kanerika, GuruSup) publishing deep-dives on multi-agent orchestration as the next scaling frontier. Google Cloud released official AI Agent Trends 2026 report. Gartner: AI software market reaching $297B by 2027.
keyword_rationale.competition_level: medium-high — many service providers writing but few truly technical deep dives
keyword_rationale.target_audience: CTOs, engineering leads, AI developers evaluating multi-agent frameworks for production
keyword_rationale.content_gap: Most existing content is high-level vendor marketing. Opportunity for a hands-on architectural comparison with decision framework.

sources.title: 2026 is the Year of Multi-Agent Architectures
AI Agent Orchestration in 2026: What Enterprises Need to Know

output.article_angle: From Single-Agent to Multi-Agent Architecture: A Practical Guide to Building Production-Grade AI Systems in 2026

next_steps.action: Write 3000-4000 word SEO-optimized article on multi-agent architecture. Cover: orchestrator-worker pattern, framework comparison (LangGraph vs CrewAI vs OpenAI SDK vs Google ADK), enterprise deployment patterns, and a decision framework for choosing the right architecture.
Review article for keyword density, readability, structure, and SEO score. Approve or return with revisions.

hive_mind_signal.content: Keyword 'multi-agent architecture' selected for first pipeline run. High search volume (27K+/mo for LangGraph alone), 2026 trend momentum, clear content gap for technical architectural comparison. Writing article next.
